articles.title: PAXOS AIMS FOR NATIONAL BANK CHARTER
articles.summary: Paxos is applying for a national trust bank charter with the OCC, joining Ripple and Circle in seeking federal oversight as the GENIUS Act reshapes stablecoin regulation. The issuer of PayPal USD and Pax Dollar says the move will reinforce its commitment to safety and transparency, but comes just a week after settling with NYDFS over past AML and due diligence lapses tied to its Binance partnership.

articles.title: ETH TOPS BTC IN YTD GAINS AMID ETF AND TREASURY DEMAND
articles.summary: ETH has overtaken BTC in year-to-date gains, rising 29% compared to Bitcoin's 28%, as institutional demand pushes Ethereum above $4,000 for the first time since December. The increase has been sparked by $5B in recent inflows to spot ETH ETFs and growing allocations from digital asset treasury firms mimicking Strategy's Bitcoin playbook.

articles.title: STRIPE BUILDING ‘TEMPO' BLOCKCHAIN WITH CRYPTO VC PARADIGM
articles.summary: Stripe is developing a payments-focused layer-1 blockchain called “Tempo,” per a now-removed product marketing job posting that described a stealth five-person team working with Paradigm. The chain is rumored to be EVM-compatible, and follows Stripe's recent stablecoin push, including the $1.1B acquisition of Bridge and the purchase of wallet developer Privy, positioning Stripe to own more of the stablecoin stack from issuance to transaction processing.

articles.title: SHARPLINK RAISES $900M TO BACK ETH STRATEGY
articles.summary: SharpLink secured nearly $900 million in new capital last week, signaling strong market conviction in its Ethereum-focused treasury strategy. The raise highlights growing institutional alignment around ETH's long-term role in digital finance.

articles.title: STRING IT TOGETHER: HOW STABLECOINS GO MAINSTREAM
articles.summary: Stablecoins can achieve mainstream US consumer payments adoption by repeating credit cards' two-step path: first win niche segments on intrinsic advantages like comparative convenience and powerful incentives, then connect those “fiefdoms” via an open, standardized, participant-owned network. Near-term wedges include cross-border shoppers underserved by dollar rails and incentive-first programs where merchants launch transferable, whitelabeled stablecoins funded by float yield, banks' ability to custody stables, and UX improvements like account abstraction and gas sponsorship. Programmability enables novel rewards and financing models that can pull balances into stables and drive spend if checkout is seamless. To scale, stablecoins need a neutral, user-owned network to enforce interoperability, compliance, and consumer protections across chains and issuers.

articles.title: REFLECTIONS, RANT, AND ROAD AHEAD FOR CRYPTO X AI AGENTS
articles.summary: Phase 1 of crypto x AI saw speculative euphoria with a market cap topping $10B, followed by a collapse where liquidity rotated out, tokens fell 50-90%, teams went silent, and “L1 for agents” frameworks/launchpads plus chat UIs failed to deliver production utility. Today, chat front ends remain too slow/error-prone for transactions, and many Solana AI tokens trade as memecoins, but improving agentic models, MCP standardization, and walleted agents are emerging as workable primitives. Over the next 6-12 months, viable use cases are expected in complex agentic workflows rather than simple swaps. In 1+ years, agents could drive stablecoin payments with x402, enable crypto-native revenue sharing, spur AI-embedded protocols, verifiability/coordination networks, and an on-chain personal context layer.

articles.title: DESIGN TESTNETS FOR FEEDBACK, NOT FARMING
articles.summary: Fluent is launching a testnet experience designed around collecting app feedback rather than traditional "chain farming," arguing that current testnets have devolved into spam-filled environments where users perform mindless tasks hoping for token rewards. The company criticizes how chains typically optimize for vanity metrics like transaction volume and user counts instead of fostering the development of useful applications that drive long-term success. Fluent's approach focuses on helping builders iterate toward product-market fit by systematically collecting and analyzing user feedback across areas like usability, engagement, and performance. This "apps first" philosophy positions chains as infrastructure that succeeds when the applications built on them succeed, similar to how AWS benefits from Netflix's success.

articles.title: STRIPE'S STABLECOIN STRATEGY CULMINATES IN NEW L1
articles.summary: Stripe's stablecoin journey has rapidly evolved from basic onramps in 2022 to launching its own L1 blockchain, “Tempo,” in 2025. After acquiring Bridge Stablecoin and Privy for over $1B each, Stripe is now positioned to vertically integrate payments, wallets, and chain infrastructure.

articles.title: COINBASE VENTURES HOLDING TON TOKEN
articles.summary: Coinbase Ventures has taken a liquid token position in the TON token, which is the canonical blockchain for Telegram.

articles.title: HIGHEST-PAYING CRYPTO JOBS
articles.summary: Crypto x AI jobs and infrastructure projects pay the most of any sub-industry in crypto, with median salaries of $400k and $288k, respectively.

articles.title: ETH FUTURES VOLUME HITS ALL-TIME HIGH ON CME
articles.summary: CME's Ethereum futures hit a record $118B in trading volume for July, up 82% month-over-month, with open interest jumping 75% to $5.21B.

articles.title: DO KWON MAY PLEAD GUILTY IN U.S. FRAUD CASE
articles.summary: Kwon faces multiple charges, including wire fraud, securities fraud, and market manipulation.
